interpretation.carrier: deterministic 10-coordinate lasso-character chart derived from the v22 full relation pairing
interpretation.hesse_transform: classical ternary-cubic Hessian applied to each sector point in F_p^10
interpretation.invariant_cut: ten Plucker-linear Hesse equations n(p_ij)=0, equivalent to R_tube(X,H_tube(X),-)=0 in this finite chart
interpretation.pencil: <X_i, H_tube(X_i)> in G(1,9) for each of the 39 Drinfeld sectors

verified_claims: The v22 quantum-character/lasso trace table admits a deterministic 10-coordinate Hesse carrier chart over F_1000003.
The 39 Drinfeld sectors give 39 distinct projective cubic points in that chart.
For every sector, the cubic Hessian is nonzero and spans a two-dimensional Hesse pencil with the original cubic.
For every sector, H(H(X)) lies in the pencil <X,H(X)>.
For every sector, all 210 Plucker quadrics vanish for the line <X,H(X)> in G(1,9).
For every sector, all ten Hesse linear equations n(p_ij)=0 vanish; equivalently the finite R_tube contraction vanishes.
The 39 sector pencils are distinct and their Plucker vectors span the full 35-dimensional Hesse linear cut inside Λ²(F^10).
The Hesse-cut dimension 35 matches the independently certified A12 surface trace algebra dimension from v22.
